question: Configure your global Git user name and email to match your first and last name (use environment variables FIRST_NAME and LAST_NAME).
hint: Use `git config --global user.name "$FIRST_NAME $LAST_NAME"` and `git config --global user.email "${FIRST_NAME,,}.${LAST_NAME,,}@example.com"`.

question: Create a private GitHub repository named exactly iac-ansible-2526-<first_name>-<last_name> (using the FIRST_NAME and LAST_NAME environment variables) with a README initialized.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
FIRST="${FIRST_NAME:-}"
LAST="${LAST_NAME:-}"
if [ -z "$FIRST" ] || [ -z "$LAST" ]; then exit 1; fi
REPO_NAME="iac-ansible-2526-${FIRST}-${LAST}"
OUTPUT=$(gh repo view "$REPO_NAME" --json name,private 2>/dev/null)
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then exit 1; fi
NAME=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r .name)
PRIVATE=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r .private)
if [ "$NAME" != "$REPO_NAME" ] || [ "$PRIVATE" != "true" ]; then exit 1; fi
exit 0

question: Add a README.md file to the repository you just created (if not already present) containing a header with the repository name, then commit and push the change.
hint: Clone the repo, create/edit README.md, git add, git commit -m 'Add README', git push.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
FIRST="${FIRST_NAME:-}"
LAST="${LAST_NAME:-}"
if [ -z "$FIRST" ] || [ -z "$LAST" ]; then exit 1; fi
REPO_NAME="iac-ansible-2526-${FIRST}-${LAST}"
OWNER=$(gh api user -q .login)
SHA=$(gh api "/repos/${OWNER}/${REPO_NAME}/contents/README.md" -q '.sha' 2>/dev/null)
if [ -z "$SHA" ] || [ "$SHA" = "null" ]; then exit 1; fi
exit 0

question: Add the lecturer (username p127280) as a collaborator with push permission to your private repository.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
FIRST="${FIRST_NAME:-}"
LAST="${LAST_NAME:-}"
if [ -z "$FIRST" ] || [ -z "$LAST" ]; then exit 1; fi
REPO_NAME="iac-ansible-2526-${FIRST}-${LAST}"
OWNER=$(gh api user -q .login)
PERM=$(gh api "/repos/${OWNER}/${REPO_NAME}/collaborators/p127280" -q '.permission' 2>/dev/null)
if [ "$PERM" != "push" ]; then exit 1; fi
exit 0
